serStmt.serInfo: Statistics South Africa. Domestic Tourism Survey 2013 [dataset]. Version 1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2014. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2015. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/tmpg-ct73

verStmt.version: v1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: Version 1 of the Domestic Tourism Survey 2013 was downloaded from Statistics South Africa's website on 14 September 2015.

stdyInfo.abstract: Statistics South Africa collects data on foreign tourism from the South African Department of Home Affairs. Data on domestic tourism is also needed to measure its contribution to the national economy. The Domestic Tourism Survey (DTS) is aimed at addressing this need by collecting data on the travel behaviour and expenditure of South African residents travelling within and outside the borders of South Africa. This survey provides data on domestic tourism activity during the period January to December 2013.

sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation covered by the data is municipality.
sumDscr.anlyUnit: The units of analysis in the Domestic Tourism Survey are households and individuals
sumDscr.universe: The target population of the survey consists of all private households and residents in workers' hostels in the nine provinces of South Africa. The survey does not cover other collective living quarters such as students' hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes: The scope of the Domestic Tourism Survey 2013 includes: household characteristcs, education of household members, tourism employment, and day/overnight trips by the respondent and/or other household members. These include travel for business, recreation, entertainment, sports and nature based travel, religious and medical travel, type of transport used and expenditure on this type of travel.

dataColl.sampProc: For the Domestic Tourism Survey SSA used a household survey master sample of 3 080 primary sampling units from the 80 787 enumeration areas (EAs) created for the 2001 Population Census. The master sample used a two-stage, stratified design with probability-proportional-to-size (PPS) sampling of PSUs from within strata, and systematic sampling of dwelling units (DUs) from the sampled primary sampling units (PSUs). A self-weighting design at provincial level was used and MS stratification was divided into two levels, primary and secondary stratification. Primary stratification was defined by metropolitan and non-metropolitan geographic area type. During secondary stratification, the Census 2001 data were summarised at PSU level. The following variables were used for secondary stratification; household size, education, occupancy status, gender, industry and income.
dataColl.resInstru: Household Questionnaire: This includes sections on: Household characteristcs, household listing, education, tourism employment, trips taken, day trips, overnight trips, barriers to taking trips, business and professional trips, recreation entertainment, sports trips, nature based trips, religious trips, medical trips, type of transport, expenditure on trips, social activites
dataColl.weight: Sampling weights for the data collected from the sampled households are constructed so that responses can be expanded appropriately to represent the entire population of South Africa. The weights are the result of calculations involving several factors, including design weights, adjustment for non-response, and benchmarking to known population estimates. The final survey weights are constructed by calibrating the adjusted base weight to the known population counts at national and provincial levels, cross-classified by 5-year age groups (see Statistical Release pp 49-50), gender and race. The calibrated weights are constructed to ensure that all persons in a household have the same final weight (integrated weighting).

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. Domestic Tourism Survey 2013 [dataset]. Version 1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2014. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2015. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/tmpg-ct73
useStmt.conditions: Public use data, available to all

fileTxt.fileCont: The DTS Daytrip 2013_v1 data file contains variables from the Flap, section 2 and section 7 in the survey questionnaire
The DTS Overnight 2013_v1 data file contains variables from section 5 and section 6 in the survey questionnaire

catgry.labl: Living together like husband and wife
Trips within 40 km radius/family relatives stays within 40 km radius
Too expensive/ I would rather spend money on something else
No family/friends to visit somewhere else
No interest/nothing to see or do that appeals to me

var.name: Q15BMNTH
var.labl: Month in which trip the respondent took the trip

var.name: Q263RSPECT
var.labl: Attended a sporting event as a spectator

var.name: Q263OSPECT
var.labl: Other Attended a sporting event as a spectator

var.name: Q263OPART
var.labl: Other Participated in a sporting event

var.name: Q267RMEDI
var.labl: Medical e.g treatment in clinic/ hospital

var.name: Q267OMEDI
var.labl: Other Medical e.g treatment in clinic/ hospital

var.name: Q211OTHRC
var.labl: Other items while on the trip

var.name: Q211TOTC
var.labl: Total spent while on the trip

catgry.labl: Dwelling/house or brick/concrete block structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling/servants’ quarters/granny flat

var.name: Q76_GYSER
var.labl: Hot running water fron a geyser

var.name: Q16ADAYTRIP
var.labl: Most recent trip by other household members

var.name: Q16BMNTH
var.labl: Month in which other HH members took the trip

var.name: Q212AINFO
var.labl: Tourism info when planning the trip

var.name: Q212EATTR
var.labl: Value for money at tourism attraction

var.name: Q212FSITE
var.labl: Cultural, historical and heritage sites and activities

var.name: Q212GNATU
var.labl: Natural attractions such as beaches,, mountains scenery etc.

catgry.labl: Living together like husband and wife

var.name: Q42WHYNOVEN
var.labl: Why were no day trips taken

catgry.labl: Trips within 40 km radius/family relatives stays within 40 km radius

var.name: Q45AROVEN

var.labl: Month in which respondent took the trip
